title: Contracts & Paperwork Pack - Index and Legal Notice
contentHtml:
Purpose of this pack

This pack contains the standard agreements and policies used by Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops", "Provider", "we") to make its curriculum-production and learning-delivery platform (the "Platform") available to partner organisations ("Partner", "you"). Each document is a re-usable template on Synops letterhead. Partners may store executed copies in their own document repository, and Synops retains a signed copy in the platform filing cabinet.

⚠ Important legal notice - read first

These documents are templates, not legal advice. They were prepared as a starting point and must be reviewed and adapted by qualified attorneys admitted in South Africa and in the relevant United States jurisdiction before signature or use. In particular, have counsel confirm: (a) company registration, VAT/tax and entity details; (b) data-protection obligations under the Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013 ("POPIA") and applicable U.S. laws (e.g. the California Consumer Privacy Act / CPRA, and any sector laws such as FERPA where student records are involved); (c) consumer-protection rules (SA Consumer Protection Act 68 of 2008; U.S. state law); (d) electronic-signature validity (SA Electronic Communications and Transactions Act 25 of 2002 - "ECTA"; U.S. ESIGN Act and UETA); and (e) the fee, tax and currency terms. Nothing here creates a lawyer-client relationship, and no warranty is given as to legal sufficiency or currency of the law. Replace every item shown in [square brackets] with real details, and delete guidance notes marked (Note: …) before signature.

Governing-law structure

The agreements are drafted South Africa first, with a United States Annex that a U.S.-based Partner (or Synops's U.S. affiliate) can elect so the same paperwork works across both markets. Where the U.S. Annex is elected, its terms replace the corresponding South African clauses for that Partner.

What's in the pack

# Document Use it for
01 Master Services Agreement (MSA) The core contract governing all use of the Platform.
02 Partner / Reseller Agreement Where a Partner resells or delivers the Platform to its own client organisations.
03 Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) Non-binding statement of intent to begin a relationship or pilot.
04 Data Processing Agreement (DPA) POPIA + U.S. data-protection terms for personal information processed on the Platform.
05 Order Form & Pricing Schedule The commercial terms - the fees and per-person pricing formula.
06 Service Level Agreement (SLA) Uptime, support response times and service credits.
07 Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) Rules for use of the Platform.
08 Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Protecting confidential information exchanged before/around a deal.
09 Learner Terms of Use The end-user terms shown to individual learners.

Order of precedence

Unless a document says otherwise, if there is a conflict the order of precedence is: (1) the signed Order Form (05); (2) the Data Processing Agreement (04); (3) the Master Services Agreement (01) and its schedules (06, 07); (4) the Partner/Reseller Agreement (02) where applicable; (5) the Learner Terms of Use (09). The MOU (03) is superseded by the MSA once signed.

How these fit the Platform

Executed agreements can be filed and tracked inside the Platform: a Partner stores its copies in Documents & Filing in its hub, and Synops stores the master copies in the super-admin Contracts & MOUs cabinet. Fees configured in the Order Form (05) map to the Platform's billing (subscriptions and per-active-learner invoicing).

(Note: This index page is for internal orientation and is not itself a contract.)

title: Master Services Agreement
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. Must be reviewed by SA and U.S. counsel before use. Replace all [bracketed] items and delete guidance notes before signature.)

This Master Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is made on [Effective Date] between:

  1. Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd, a company incorporated in the Republic of South Africa (registration no. [•], VAT no. [•]), of [registered address] ("Synops", "Provider"); and
  2. [PARTNER LEGAL NAME], (registration/entity no. [•]) of [address] ("Partner", "Customer").

Synops and the Partner are each a "Party" and together the "Parties".

1. Definitions

  • "Platform" - Synops's cloud curriculum-production and learning-delivery platform, including its software, standards libraries, authoring tools, learning-management features and documentation, as made available to the Partner.
  • "Order Form" - a document (see Doc 05) signed by the Parties setting out the subscription, fees and commercial terms.
  • "Authorised Users" - the Partner's administrators, instructional designers, facilitators/coaches, and learners permitted to access the Platform.
  • "Partner Content" - course content, materials, data and other information the Partner or its Authorised Users upload to or create on the Platform.
  • "Personal Information" / "Personal Data" - as defined in POPIA and, where the U.S. Annex applies, applicable U.S. law; governed by the Data Processing Agreement (Doc 04).
  • "Fees" - the amounts payable under the Order Form (Doc 05).
  • "Confidential Information" - as defined in clause 10 (and/or the Mutual NDA, Doc 08).

2. The services

2.1 Synops grants the Partner a non-exclusive, non-transferable right to access and use the Platform during the Term, for the Partner's internal business and educational purposes and (where a Partner/Reseller Agreement (Doc 02) is signed) to deliver learning to the Partner's client organisations. 2.2 Synops will provide the Platform in accordance with this Agreement, the SLA (Doc 06), and the applicable Order Form. 2.3 Synops may update or improve the Platform provided it does not materially reduce core functionality during a paid Term.

3. Partner responsibilities

3.1 The Partner will: (a) use the Platform in line with the Acceptable Use Policy (Doc 07) and applicable law; (b) keep account credentials secure and be responsible for its Authorised Users' acts and omissions; (c) obtain all consents and provide all notices required for Partner Content and for the personal information it processes on the Platform; and (d) ensure its Authorised Users accept the Learner Terms of Use (Doc 09) where applicable. 3.2 The Partner is responsible for the accuracy, quality and legality of Partner Content and of the data it uploads.

4. Fees and payment

4.1 The Partner will pay the Fees set out in the Order Form (Doc 05), which contains the pricing formula, tiers and payment terms. 4.2 Except as expressly stated, Fees are non-cancellable and paid Fees are non-refundable. 4.3 All Fees are exclusive of VAT (SA) or sales/use tax (U.S.), which is added where applicable. Late amounts bear interest as stated in the Order Form.

5. Intellectual property

5.1 Platform. Synops and its licensors own all intellectual-property rights in the Platform. No rights are granted except the access rights expressly set out here. 5.2 Partner Content. The Partner (or its licensors) owns all rights in Partner Content. The Partner grants Synops a limited licence to host, process and display Partner Content solely to provide the Platform. 5.3 Feedback. Synops may use suggestions or feedback about the Platform without restriction. 5.4 Aggregated/anonymised data. Synops may use aggregated, de-identified data (that does not identify the Partner, any organisation or any individual) to operate, secure and improve the Platform and its benchmarks.

6. Data protection and security

6.1 Each Party will comply with applicable data-protection law, including POPIA (SA) and, where the U.S. Annex applies, applicable U.S. law (including FERPA where educational records are involved, and the CCPA/CPRA where applicable). 6.2 The processing of Personal Information on the Platform is governed by the Data Processing Agreement (Doc 04), which is incorporated by reference. Synops acts as Operator/Processor on the Partner's instructions in respect of such information. 6.3 Synops will maintain appropriate technical and organisational security measures, including tenant isolation, encryption in transit, access controls and audit logging, as further described in the DPA and SLA.

7. Warranties

7.1 Each Party warrants it has the authority to enter into this Agreement. 7.2 Synops warrants it will provide the Platform with reasonable skill and care and substantially in accordance with the documentation and SLA. 7.3 Disclaimer. Except as expressly stated and to the extent permitted by law (and subject to the SA Consumer Protection Act where it applies), the Platform is provided "as is" and Synops disclaims all other warranties, including fitness for a particular purpose and that the Platform will be error-free or uninterrupted. (Note: CPA implied warranties may not be excludable for certain SA customers - confirm with counsel.)

8. Limitation of liability

8.1 Neither Party is liable for indirect, incidental, special or consequential loss, or for loss of profits, revenue, goodwill or data (except direct data-restoration costs), even if advised of the possibility. 8.2 Except for (a) a Party's liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, fraud, or wilful misconduct; (b) the Partner's payment obligations; (c) a Party's breach of confidentiality; and (d) either Party's data-protection indemnities, each Party's total aggregate liability under this Agreement is limited to the Fees paid or payable by the Partner in the [12] months before the event giving rise to the claim. 8.3 Nothing limits liability that cannot be limited by law.

9. Indemnities

9.1 Synops will indemnify the Partner against third-party claims that the Platform (excluding Partner Content) infringes that third party's intellectual-property rights, subject to prompt notice, control of defence, and the limitations in clause 8. 9.2 The Partner will indemnify Synops against third-party claims arising from Partner Content, the Partner's data, or the Partner's or its Authorised Users' breach of this Agreement, the AUP or applicable law.

10. Confidentiality

10.1 Each Party will keep the other's Confidential Information confidential, use it only to perform this Agreement, and protect it with reasonable care. This clause does not apply to information that is public through no breach, independently developed, or lawfully received from a third party. The Mutual NDA (Doc 08), where signed, supplements this clause.

11. Term and termination

11.1 This Agreement starts on the Effective Date and continues for the Initial Term in the Order Form, renewing for successive periods of [12] months unless either Party gives [60] days' written notice of non-renewal. 11.2 Either Party may terminate for the other's material breach not remedied within [30] days of written notice, or immediately on the other's insolvency/business rescue. 11.3 On termination: access ends; the Partner may export Partner Content for [30] days; and Synops will delete or return Partner Content and Personal Information in line with the DPA. Accrued Fees remain payable.

12. General

12.1 Force majeure. Neither Party is liable for delay/failure due to events beyond its reasonable control. 12.2 Assignment. Neither Party may assign without the other's consent, except to an affiliate or in connection with a merger/sale of substantially all assets. 12.3 Notices. In writing to the addresses above (or as updated), including by email to the designated contacts. 12.4 Entire agreement. This Agreement, its schedules and Order Forms are the entire agreement and supersede prior discussions (including any MOU). 12.5 Severability; waiver; counterparts; electronic signature. Standard terms apply; the Parties consent to electronic execution under ECTA (SA) and ESIGN/UETA (U.S.). 12.6 Governing law and jurisdiction (South Africa - default). This Agreement is governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa, and the Parties submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the [High Court of South Africa, Gauteng Division]. Disputes will first be referred to good-faith senior-executive negotiation and, failing resolution within [30] days, to arbitration under the rules of the Arbitration Foundation of Southern Africa (AFSA) seated in [Johannesburg], in English.

United States Annex (elective)

(Note: This Annex applies only if elected in the Order Form for a U.S.-domiciled Partner or where Synops's U.S. affiliate is the contracting entity. Where elected, the following replace the corresponding South African provisions.)

US-1. Contracting entity. The Synops contracting entity is [Synops U.S. entity, e.g. Synops Consulting Group, LLC / Inc., a Delaware entity].

US-2. Data protection. References to POPIA are read as references to applicable U.S. federal and state privacy laws, including the California Consumer Privacy Act as amended by the CPRA, and FERPA where the Partner is an educational agency/institution and student education records are processed. Where FERPA applies, Synops acts as a "school official" with a legitimate educational interest, uses data only for the authorised purpose, and does not re-disclose except as permitted. The DPA's U.S. Addendum governs.

US-3. Consumer law. The SA Consumer Protection Act references do not apply; U.S. state consumer-protection law applies as relevant.

US-4. Governing law and disputes. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of [Delaware], excluding its conflict-of-laws rules. The Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts located in [New Castle County, Delaware], OR (elect one) the Parties agree to binding arbitration administered by JAMS / the American Arbitration Association (AAA) under its Commercial Rules, seated in [•], in English. Each Party waives trial by jury to the extent permitted by law.

US-5. Taxes. VAT references are read as U.S. sales/use tax; the Partner provides valid exemption certificates where applicable.

US-6. Electronic signatures. Governed by the federal ESIGN Act and the applicable state's UETA.

Signatures

For Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd For [PARTNER LEGAL NAME]
Signature: ______________________ Signature: ______________________
Name: [•] Name: [•]
Title: [•] Title: [•]
Date: [•] Date: [•]

title: Partner / Reseller Agreement
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. Review with SA and U.S. counsel, especially on reseller tax, agency and consumer-law issues. Replace [bracketed] items before signature.)

This Partner / Reseller Agreement ("Agreement") supplements the Master Services Agreement ("MSA") between Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops") and [PARTNER LEGAL NAME] ("Partner") and applies where the Partner delivers or resells the Platform to its own client organisations ("End Customers"). If there is a conflict, the MSA governs except where this Agreement expressly varies it.

1. Appointment

1.1 Synops appoints the Partner as a non-exclusive partner to market, and to [resell / deliver as a managed service], access to the Platform to End Customers in the Territory: [South Africa / United States / •]. 1.2 The Partner is an independent contractor, not Synops's agent, and may not bind Synops or make representations beyond Synops's published materials.

2. Partner obligations

2.1 The Partner will: (a) market the Platform ethically and accurately; (b) ensure each End Customer is bound by terms no less protective of Synops than the MSA, AUP and Learner Terms (a "Pass-through Terms" requirement); (c) provide first-line support to End Customers; (d) not alter, reverse-engineer or white-label the Platform except using Synops's approved branding features; and (e) comply with anti-bribery, sanctions and data-protection laws. 2.2 The Partner is responsible for its End Customer relationships, including onboarding, and remains liable to Synops for Fees attributable to its End Customers unless a direct-billing arrangement is agreed.

3. Branding and white-label

3.1 Synops grants the Partner a limited licence to use the Platform's white-label features and to display Synops's marks only as permitted in Synops's brand guidelines. All goodwill vests in Synops. The Partner will not register any confusingly similar marks or domains.

4. Fees, margin and billing

4.1 Pricing is per the Order Form / Pricing Schedule (Doc 05). The Parties will agree one of: (a) wholesale/margin model - Synops invoices the Partner at wholesale rates and the Partner sets End-Customer prices; or (b) referral/commission model - Synops bills End Customers directly and pays the Partner [•]% commission. 4.2 The Partner is responsible for its own pricing to End Customers and for any resale taxes arising from its onward supply.

5. Data protection in the reseller chain

5.1 Where the Partner determines the purposes/means of processing End-Customer personal information, the Partner is the Responsible Party/Controller and Synops is the Operator/Processor under the DPA (Doc 04); the Partner must have its own compliant terms with each End Customer and pass through equivalent processor obligations. 5.2 The Partner will ensure a lawful basis and required notices/consents for all personal information processed via its End Customers.

6. Term, termination and effect

6.1 This Agreement runs co-terminously with the MSA unless terminated earlier. 6.2 On termination, the Partner will cease marketing, and the Parties will agree an orderly transition for active End Customers (including, at Synops's option, novation of End Customers directly to Synops) to avoid disruption to learners.

7. Non-solicitation

During the Term and for [12] months after, neither Party will knowingly solicit the other's employees engaged in the relationship, save for general advertising. (Note: non-solicit/non-compete enforceability varies - SA reasonableness test; several U.S. states restrict or ban them. Confirm with counsel.)

8. Governing law

As per the MSA (South Africa by default; U.S. Annex if elected).

Signatures

For Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd For [PARTNER LEGAL NAME]
Signature: ______________________ Signature: ______________________
Name / Title: [•] Name / Title: [•]
Date: [•] Date: [•]

title: Memorandum of Understanding
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. This MOU is intended to be mostly non-binding; confirm with counsel. Replace [bracketed] items before signature.)

This Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") records the mutual intent of Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops") and [PARTNER LEGAL NAME] ("Partner") (each a "Party") to explore a working relationship regarding Synops's curriculum-production and learning-delivery platform (the "Platform").

1. Purpose

The Parties wish to [explore a pilot / scope a partnership / evaluate the Platform] with a view to entering into a definitive Master Services Agreement and Order Form. This MOU sets out their shared understanding and is a statement of intent only.

2. Areas of intended collaboration

(Edit to fit.)

  • Synops to make the Platform available for a [90]-day evaluation / pilot covering [scope, e.g. up to N learners, M courses, one organisation].
  • Partner to provide [content / SMEs / learner cohorts / feedback].
  • The Parties to jointly assess fit against the Partner's accreditation, delivery and reporting needs.
  • Indicative commercial model per the Pricing Schedule (Doc 05), to be finalised in an Order Form.

3. Roles and points of contact

Synops Partner
Lead contact [Name, title, email] [Name, title, email]
Responsibilities [•] [•]

4. Timeline (indicative)

Milestone Target date
Kick-off [•]
Pilot start [•]
Review [•]
Decision on definitive agreement [•]

5. Costs

Unless otherwise agreed in writing, each Party bears its own costs during the period of this MOU. Any pilot fees are set out in [Schedule A / an Order Form].

6. Confidentiality

Information shared under this MOU is confidential and is governed by the Mutual NDA (Doc 08) [which the Parties have signed / which the Parties will sign].

7. No exclusivity

Nothing in this MOU prevents either Party from pursuing similar discussions or arrangements with others.

8. Status of this MOU (binding effect)

8.1 This MOU is not legally binding and does not create an obligation to enter into any further agreement, except clauses 5 (Costs), 6 (Confidentiality), 8 (Status), 9 (Data protection) and 10 (Governing law), which are binding. 8.2 A binding relationship will arise only on signature of a definitive Master Services Agreement and Order Form, which will supersede this MOU.

9. Data protection

Any personal information shared during the evaluation will be handled in accordance with POPIA (SA) and applicable U.S. law, and, where personal information is processed on the Platform, the Data Processing Agreement (Doc 04).

10. Governing law

This MOU is governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa (or, where the U.S. Annex to the MSA is elected, the State of [Delaware]).

Signatures

For Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd For [PARTNER LEGAL NAME]
Signature: ______________________ Signature: ______________________
Name / Title: [•] Name / Title: [•]
Date: [•] Date: [•]

title: Data Processing Agreement
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. Data-protection terms carry real risk; have SA and U.S. privacy counsel review before use. Replace [bracketed] items before signature.)

This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") forms part of the Master Services Agreement ("MSA") between Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops", the "Operator/Processor") and [PARTNER LEGAL NAME] ("Partner", the "Responsible Party/Controller"). It governs the processing of personal information on the Platform.

1. Definitions and roles

1.1 Terms such as "Personal Information", "Processing", "Responsible Party", "Operator", "Data Subject" and "Special Personal Information" have the meanings in POPIA. Under the U.S. Addendum, equivalent terms in applicable U.S. law apply. 1.2 The Partner is the Responsible Party (it decides why and how personal information is processed) and Synops is the Operator (it processes on the Partner's documented instructions to provide the Platform).

2. Scope of processing (POPIA s.19-21)

Item Detail
Subject-matter Provision of the Platform
Duration The Term of the MSA + retention period below
Nature & purpose Hosting, storage, delivery and analytics of learning; account and progress management
Categories of Data Subjects Partner staff, instructional designers, facilitators/coaches, and learners
Categories of Personal Information Names, contact details, role, login/usage records, course progress, assessment results, credentials, and [optional demographic/SETA/funder fields]
Special Personal Information Only if the Partner configures it (e.g. disability/accommodation data) - processed strictly on instruction
Children's information [Yes/No] - if learners may be minors, see clause 8

3. Synops's obligations as Operator (POPIA s.20-21)

Synops will: (a) process Personal Information only on the Partner's documented instructions, including as to cross-border transfers, unless required by law (in which case it will notify the Partner unless legally prohibited); (b) maintain the confidentiality of Personal Information and ensure persons authorised to process it are bound by confidentiality; (c) implement appropriate, reasonable technical and organisational security measures (Annex A) to secure the integrity and confidentiality of Personal Information and prevent loss, damage or unauthorised access, as required by POPIA s.19; (d) assist the Partner, taking into account the nature of processing, to respond to Data Subject requests and to meet its POPIA obligations (security, breach notification, impact assessments); (e) notify the Partner without undue delay (and in any event within [48] hours) after becoming aware of a security compromise affecting Personal Information, with information to enable the Partner to meet its POPIA s.22 notification duties to the Information Regulator and affected Data Subjects; and (f) make available information necessary to demonstrate compliance and allow for audits (clause 6).

4. Sub-operators (sub-processors)

4.1 The Partner [generally authorises / must specifically authorise] Synops's use of sub-operators (e.g. cloud hosting, email delivery). Synops maintains a current list at [link] and will give [30] days' notice of intended changes, allowing the Partner to object on reasonable data-protection grounds. 4.2 Synops will impose data-protection obligations on each sub-operator that are no less protective than this DPA and remains responsible for their performance.

5. Cross-border transfers (POPIA s.72)

5.1 Personal Information may be hosted/processed in [data-centre location(s), e.g. South Africa / EU / United States]. Where Personal Information is transferred outside South Africa, Synops will ensure a lawful basis under POPIA s.72 (e.g. the recipient is subject to adequate protection, or the transfer is necessary to perform the contract, or the Data Subject consents). Where the U.S. Addendum applies, transfers comply with applicable U.S. requirements.

6. Audit

Synops will, on reasonable notice and no more than [once per year] (or after a compromise), make available compliance information and, subject to confidentiality, permit the Partner (or its independent auditor) to verify compliance; third-party certifications/reports (e.g. [ISO 27001 / SOC 2], if held) may satisfy this.

7. Return and deletion

On termination or on the Partner's request, Synops will delete or return Personal Information (at the Partner's choice) within [30] days, and delete existing copies unless retention is required by law. The Partner may export data via the Platform during the post-termination window (MSA clause 11).

8. Children / student data

8.1 Where learners may be minors, the Partner is responsible for obtaining any required consent from a competent person/parent under POPIA s.34-35, and Synops will process such information strictly on instruction. 8.2 Under the U.S. Addendum, where the Partner is an educational institution, FERPA applies and Synops acts as a "school official" (see Addendum); where services are directed to children under 13, COPPA obligations are the Partner's responsibility and Synops will support compliant configurations.

9. Liability

Liability for breaches of this DPA is subject to the MSA's limitation of liability, provided that data-protection statutory liabilities that cannot be limited by law are not excluded.

Annex A - Security measures (summary)

Tenant isolation with database-level access controls; encryption of data in transit (TLS) [and at rest]; role-based access control and least privilege; audit logging of administrative and impersonation actions; secure credential handling (hashed passwords); backup and recovery; vulnerability management and patching; personnel confidentiality and access reviews; incident-response process. (Note: describe your actual controls; align with SLA Doc 06.)

Annex B - United States Addendum (elective)

(Applies where elected in the Order Form or where U.S. law governs the relevant data.)

B-1. CCPA/CPRA (California). For personal information subject to the CCPA/CPRA, Synops acts as a "Service Provider": it processes personal information only to perform the services and on the Partner's ("Business's") instructions; it will not sell or share personal information, and will not retain, use or disclose it for any purpose other than the services or as permitted by law; it will not combine it with data from other sources except as permitted; and it certifies it understands and will comply with these restrictions. Synops will assist the Business with consumer-rights requests.

B-2. FERPA (education records). Where the Partner is an educational agency/institution, "education records" and personally identifiable information are disclosed to Synops as a "school official" with a legitimate educational interest; Synops uses such data only for the authorised purpose, is under the Partner's direct control regarding use and maintenance, and will not re-disclose except as FERPA permits or the Partner directs.

B-3. State breach-notification laws. Synops will support the Partner's compliance with applicable U.S. state data-breach-notification statutes, in addition to POPIA s.22 where relevant.

Signatures

For Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd For [PARTNER LEGAL NAME]
Signature: ______________________ Signature: ______________________
Name / Title: [•] Name / Title: [•]
Date: [•] Date: [•]

title: Order Form & Pricing Schedule
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal or financial advice. Have counsel and a finance professional confirm the fees, tax treatment and currency terms. Example rates below are illustrative placeholders; replace with your agreed figures.)

This Order Form is entered into under, and forms part of, the Master Services Agreement ("MSA") between Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops") and the Partner named below. If there is a conflict between this Order Form and the MSA, this Order Form governs for the commercial terms it covers.

1. Parties and order details

Field Detail
Partner (legal name) [PARTNER LEGAL NAME]
Registration / entity no. [•]
VAT / tax no. [•]
Billing contact [Name, email, phone]
Billing address [•]
Order (Effective) Date [•]
Initial Term [12 / 24 / 36] months
Billing currency ☐ ZAR (South African Rand) ☐ USD (US Dollar)
Billing cycle ☐ Monthly in arrears ☐ Annual in advance (with monthly true-up)

2. The pricing formula

The recurring monthly charge, excluding tax, is:

M = B + L - D + A

where:

  • B - Platform Base Fee. A fixed monthly fee per Partner tenant for access to the Platform (multi-tenant SaaS, white-label branding, standard support). Set by the plan chosen in §4.
  • L - Per-Active-Learner Fee. The "per person use" component. L = Σ (nₜ × rₜ) across the pricing tiers, applied marginally (each learner is charged at the rate for the tier their position falls in - like a graduated tariff), where nₜ is the number of Active Learners falling in tier t and rₜ is that tier's per-learner rate (§3).
  • D - Discounts. Annual/multi-year commitment discount, plus any funder/public-benefit or promotional discount agreed in §5.
  • A - Add-ons. Optional recurring extras (e.g. additional storage, premium support, extra environments) selected in §6.

The amount payable is then:

Total Payable = M × (1 + tax rate)

Tax is VAT at 15% for South African invoices (SA VAT Act), or US sales/use tax as applicable for U.S. invoices (Partner is responsible for exemption certificates where relevant).

One-time and usage charges (billed separately from M):

One-time at kickoff = I (Implementation) + (PS days × day-rate)

and, if the Partner elects certification-based billing instead of per-learner: an optional Per-Credential Fee per accredited certificate issued (§7).

Definitions used in the formula

  • "Active Learner" - a unique learner account that accesses at least one course, activity or assessment on the Platform during the billing month. Accounts that are merely provisioned but never used in the month are not counted. Active-Learner counts are measured by the Platform's own usage metering, and reconciled at least annually.
  • "Provisioned Seat" (alternative model) - a learner account made available regardless of use. Elect this in §4 if you prefer a fixed committed-seat model rather than usage-based billing.

3. Per-Active-Learner tier table (rₜ)

(Marginal tiering. Example placeholder rates - edit to your agreed pricing.)

Tier Active Learners in the month Rate - ZAR / learner / month Rate - USD / learner / month
1 1 - 100 [60.00] [3.20]
2 101 - 500 [48.00] [2.60]
3 501 - 2,000 [38.00] [2.05]
4 2,001 - 5,000 [30.00] [1.60]
5 5,001 + [24.00] [1.30]

4. Plan and Base Fee (B)

Plan Includes Base Fee - ZAR / month Base Fee - USD / month
Essential Core Platform, 1 tenant, standard support, standard storage [7,500] [400]
Growth Essential + advanced reporting, priority support, SSO [14,000] [750]
Scale Growth + dedicated environment, custom SLA, named CSM [26,000] [1,400]

Selected plan: ☐ Essential ☐ Growth ☐ Scale Billing model: ☐ Per-Active-Learner ☐ Provisioned Seat

5. Discounts (D)

Discount Basis Value
Annual prepayment Pay the full Initial Term annually in advance [10] %
Multi-year commitment 24-month term [10]% · 36-month term [15]% [•] %
Funder / public-benefit / NGO At Synops's discretion, on evidence of status (e.g. SETA/NPO/Title IV context) up to [20] %
Promotional / launch As agreed in writing [•] %

6. Add-ons (A)

Add-on Unit ZAR USD
Additional storage per 100 GB / month [750] [40]
Premium support (extended hours) per month [4,500] [250]
Additional (sandbox/staging) environment per month [3,000] [160]
SETA/QCTO or accreditor export pack per month [2,000] [110]

7. One-time and professional-services fees

Item Unit ZAR USD
Implementation & onboarding (I) one-time [45,000] [2,500]
Data migration per source system [15,000] [850]
Professional services / bespoke instructional design (day-rate) per consultant-day [9,500] [550]
Per-Credential Fee (optional, certification-only model) per accredited certificate issued [25.00] [1.35]

8. Worked example (illustrative)

Partner on the Essential plan, 320 Active Learners in the month, annual prepayment (10%), ZAR, VAT 15%:

Step Calculation ZAR
Base Fee (B) Essential 7,500.00
Tier 1 (1-100) 100 × 60.00 6,000.00
Tier 2 (101-320) 220 × 48.00 10,560.00
Per-Learner subtotal (L) 6,000 + 10,560 16,560.00
M before discount B + L 24,060.00
Discount (D) 10% annual prepay -2,406.00
M (excl. VAT) 21,654.00
VAT 15% 3,248.10
Total payable / month M × 1.15 24,902.10
Annualised (× 12) 298,825.20

(USD works identically using the USD column, with U.S. sales tax substituted for VAT where applicable.)

9. Billing, true-up and payment terms

  1. Measurement. Active-Learner counts are taken from the Platform's usage metering for each calendar month.
  2. Invoicing. Monthly in arrears, or annually in advance with a monthly/quarterly true-up where actual Active Learners exceed the committed band. Over-committed prepayments are not automatically refunded but may be carried to the next period by agreement.
  3. Payment. Due within [30] days of invoice date. Amounts are exclusive of tax, which is added where applicable.
  4. Late payment. Interest accrues on overdue amounts at [the SA prime rate + 2% / the maximum lawful rate] (SA) or 1.5% per month or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower (U.S.).
  5. Currency & FX. Invoices are issued in the billing currency selected in §1. Cross-border taxes, withholding and bank charges are the Partner's responsibility unless stated otherwise.
  6. Annual adjustment. Synops may adjust list rates once per year on [30-60] days' notice, capped at [CPI / 8]% for renewing Partners during a committed term.

10. Signatures

Title: [•] Title: [•]
Date: [•] Date: [•]

title: Service Level Agreement (SLA)
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - align targets and credits with what you can actually deliver. Review with counsel. Replace [bracketed] items.)

This SLA forms part of the Master Services Agreement between Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops") and [PARTNER LEGAL NAME] ("Partner").

1. Availability commitment

1.1 Synops will use commercially reasonable efforts to make the Platform available at [99.5]% measured monthly ("Monthly Uptime"). 1.2 Monthly Uptime = ((Total minutes in month - Downtime minutes) ÷ Total minutes in month) × 100, where Downtime excludes: (a) scheduled maintenance (clause 2); (b) events beyond Synops's reasonable control (force majeure); (c) issues caused by the Partner, its content, or third-party networks; and (d) suspension for breach or non-payment.

2. Scheduled maintenance

Synops will give at least [48] hours' notice of planned maintenance and will target low-usage windows ([e.g. 22:00-02:00 SAST]). Emergency maintenance may occur with shorter notice.

3. Support

Priority Definition Target response
P1 - Critical Platform down or major feature unusable for many users [2] business hours
P2 - High Significant feature impaired; workaround difficult [1] business day
P3 - Normal Minor issue or question; workaround exists [3] business days
P4 - Low Cosmetic / enhancement request [best efforts]

3.1 Support hours: [08:00-17:00 SAST, Mon-Fri, excluding SA public holidays]; extended/24×7 support available as a paid add-on (Doc 05). 3.2 Support channels: [email / in-app / portal at •].

4. Service credits

4.1 If Monthly Uptime falls below the commitment, the Partner may claim a service credit against the next invoice, on written request within [30] days:

Monthly Uptime Service credit (% of that month's Base Fee)
< 99.5% but ≥ 99.0% [5]%
< 99.0% but ≥ 95.0% [10]%
< 95.0% [25]%

4.2 Service credits are the Partner's sole and exclusive remedy for availability shortfalls, capped at [25]% of the affected month's Base Fee.

5. Data backup and recovery

Synops performs regular backups and targets a recovery point objective (RPO) of [24 hours] and recovery time objective (RTO) of [8 hours] for a major incident. Backups are not a substitute for the Partner's own export/records duties.

6. Security and status

Synops maintains the security measures described in the DPA (Doc 04, Annex A) and will notify the Partner of material incidents per the DPA. Status and incident updates are provided via [status page / email].

(This SLA is a schedule to the MSA and shares its governing law and signature.)

title: Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. Review with counsel. This AUP is a schedule to the MSA and applies to all Authorised Users.)

This Acceptable Use Policy governs use of the Synops curriculum and learning Platform (the "Platform"). It applies to [PARTNER LEGAL NAME] and all of its Authorised Users (administrators, instructional designers, facilitators/coaches and learners).

1. Permitted use

The Platform may be used only for lawful curriculum production, learning delivery, assessment and related administration, in line with the MSA and applicable law.

2. Prohibited conduct

Users must not:

  1. upload, post or transmit content that is unlawful, defamatory, harassing, hateful, or infringing of others' intellectual-property or privacy rights;
  2. upload malware or attempt to gain unauthorised access to, disrupt, or probe the Platform, other tenants, or its security (no penetration testing without Synops's prior written consent);
  3. reverse-engineer, decompile, scrape at scale, or copy the Platform's software or non-Partner content except as the law expressly permits;
  4. use the Platform to send spam or unsolicited communications, or to build a competing product;
  5. misrepresent identity, share credentials, or circumvent access controls, usage metering or licensing limits;
  6. upload personal information without a lawful basis and required notices/consents, or upload special/children's information except as configured and lawful;
  7. use the Platform for high-risk activities where failure could lead to death, personal injury or environmental damage; or
  8. violate export-control or sanctions laws.

3. Content responsibility

The Partner is responsible for all Partner Content and for the accuracy, legality and accreditation-fitness of the courses it builds. Synops does not pre-vet Partner Content.

4. Fair use and metering

Usage is metered for billing (Doc 05). Attempts to obscure Active-Learner counts or otherwise defeat metering are a breach.

5. Enforcement

Synops may investigate suspected breaches and may suspend access (in whole or part) where necessary to protect the Platform, other customers or third parties, or to comply with law, giving notice where practicable. Repeated or serious breaches may lead to termination under the MSA.

6. Reporting

Report suspected abuse or security issues to [security@synops-consulting.com].

(This AUP is a schedule to the MSA and shares its governing law.)

title: Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. Review with counsel. Replace [bracketed] items before signature.)

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("NDA") is made on [Effective Date] between Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops") and [PARTNER LEGAL NAME] ("Partner") (each a "Party", and in respect of any disclosure a "Disclosing Party" or "Receiving Party").

1. Purpose

The Parties wish to exchange confidential information to evaluate and pursue a potential or actual business relationship regarding Synops's Platform (the "Purpose").

2. Confidential Information

"Confidential Information" means non-public information disclosed by or on behalf of a Disclosing Party, in any form, that is marked or would reasonably be understood to be confidential - including business, technical, financial, product, security, customer, learner and personal information, pricing, and the existence and terms of discussions.

3. Obligations

The Receiving Party will: (a) use Confidential Information only for the Purpose; (b) protect it with at least reasonable care and no less than it protects its own similar information; (c) disclose it only to its personnel and professional advisers who need it for the Purpose and are bound by confidentiality; and (d) not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent.

4. Exclusions

Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes public without breach; (b) was rightfully known before disclosure; (c) is independently developed without use of the Confidential Information; or (d) is rightfully received from a third party without duty of confidentiality.

5. Compelled disclosure

If legally compelled to disclose, the Receiving Party may do so provided it gives (where lawful) prompt notice and reasonable cooperation to allow the Disclosing Party to seek protection, and discloses only what is required.

6. Personal information

Any personal information exchanged is handled in accordance with POPIA (SA) and applicable U.S. law; where personal information is processed on the Platform, the DPA (Doc 04) governs.

7. Term and return

7.1 This NDA applies to disclosures made during [2] years from the Effective Date; confidentiality obligations survive for [3] years after disclosure (and, for trade secrets, for as long as they remain trade secrets). 7.2 On request, the Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information, subject to legal-retention and routine backup exceptions.

8. No licence, no warranty

No licence or intellectual-property right is granted except the limited right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose. Confidential Information is provided "as is" with no warranty of accuracy.

9. General

Injunctive relief is available for breach given the risk of irreparable harm. This NDA is governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa (or the State of [Delaware] where the U.S. Annex is elected), with jurisdiction as in the MSA. The Parties consent to electronic signature and counterparts.

title: Learner Terms of Use
contentHtml:
(Note: Template only - not legal advice. Review with counsel, especially minors, consumer law and privacy notices. These terms are shown to individual learners; keep them plain-language.)

Welcome. These Learner Terms of Use ("Terms") govern your use of the learning platform (the "Platform") provided by Synops Consulting Group (Pty) Ltd ("Synops", "we") and made available to you through your organisation ("your Organisation"). By accessing the Platform you agree to these Terms.

1. Your account

Your account is provided through your Organisation. Keep your login details secure, don't share them, and tell your Organisation or us if you suspect misuse. You're responsible for activity under your account.

2. Acceptable use

Use the Platform only for your learning. Don't: break the law; upload harmful, unlawful or infringing content; harass others; attempt to access other people's accounts or our systems; or cheat or misrepresent your assessment results. Full rules are in the Acceptable Use Policy.

3. Your content

You keep ownership of what you create (e.g. submissions, discussion posts). You grant us and your Organisation a licence to host and display it to run the course and assess your work.

4. Your privacy

We process your personal information (such as your name, progress, and assessment results) to provide the Platform, on the instructions of your Organisation, which is the party responsible for your data. Our handling is governed by the Data Processing Agreement with your Organisation and applicable law (POPIA in South Africa; applicable U.S. laws including FERPA where relevant). For details about your data and your rights, contact your Organisation first. You may have rights to access, correct or delete your information as the law allows.

5. Minors

If you are under 18 (or the age of majority where you live), you may use the Platform only with the consent of a parent, guardian or your Organisation acting appropriately, as arranged by your Organisation.

6. Credentials

Certificates or credentials you earn reflect completion/assessment recorded on the Platform. They do not by themselves confer accreditation, licensure or employment; the value of a credential depends on your Organisation and the relevant accreditor.

7. Availability and changes

We aim to keep the Platform available but can't guarantee it will always be uninterrupted or error-free. We may update the Platform and these Terms; material changes will be communicated through your Organisation or the Platform.

8. Disclaimers and liability

The Platform is provided "as is" to the extent permitted by law. To the maximum extent the law allows (and subject to any non-excludable consumer rights), we are not liable for indirect or consequential loss, or for loss arising from your Organisation's decisions about your learning or records.

9. Ending your access

Your access is controlled by your Organisation and may end when your enrolment ends or your Organisation's agreement ends. You can ask your Organisation to close your account.

10. Governing law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa, or - where your Organisation's agreement elects the U.S. Annex - the State of [Delaware], without limiting mandatory local consumer protections that apply to you.

Questions? Contact your Organisation's administrator, or Synops at [support@synops-consulting.com].
